The parent does not establish a binding mechanism (e.g., regulation/ordinance) or a defined spatial scope (e.g., statewide/citywide), so sub-actions cannot inherit enforceability or geography, though Action 3 does create a concrete policy requirement within CP-36. Coverage is high because the actions span multiple workforce pipelines and professional groups relevant to forest carbon practices and wood-product utilization; coherence is high because the actions target distinct audiences and reinforce one another as a delivery chain (education → professional requirements/certifications → sector outreach).
